key_insight: PluraMath shows that multilingual mathematical reasoning still drops sharply for underrepresented languages, even in strong modern LLMs.
engineering_relevance: The benchmark helps engineers evaluate and improve reasoning models for real-world multilingual deployment beyond English and Chinese.
method: Extended PolyMath with 18 additional underrepresented languages across 6 language families.
Used a human-curated pipeline where native speakers validated pre-computed translations.
Benchmarked 27 reasoning LLMs across small, mid-size, large, and closed-source ensemble settings.
evidence_signal: The study reports persistent performance gaps between high-resource and underrepresented languages, with better results linked to stronger instruction-following ability.
watch_item: Because the dataset relies on translated items, translation quality and language-specific ambiguity can still affect measured reasoning performance.
paper_title: PluraMath: Extending Mathematical Reasoning Evaluation Beyond High-Resource Languages
